title: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 1
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

A

For more than a century, movie theatres were among the most visible entertainment buildings in towns and cities. Many early cinemas were built as showpieces, with bright signs, decorated ceilings and large foyers designed to make an evening at the pictures feel special. Later, suburban multiplexes replaced many older single-screen theatres, offering several films under one roof and large car parks nearby. Today, however, even some multiplexes are under pressure. In many countries, cinema attendance has not returned to the levels seen before the pandemic, and the habit of watching new films at home has become stronger.

B

The decline did not begin with streaming. Television reduced cinema-going in the mid-twentieth century, and home video later gave audiences another reason to stay away. What streaming changed was convenience. Viewers no longer needed to rent a disc or wait for a scheduled broadcast; they could choose from a large catalogue immediately. During periods when cinemas were closed, families also invested in better screens, speakers and subscriptions. When theatres reopened, some people returned for blockbusters, but many became more selective about what deserved the cost of tickets, transport and snacks.

C

The economics of running a cinema are difficult. Operators must pay rent, electricity, staff wages, cleaning costs and fees to film distributors. Projectors, sound systems and seats also require regular maintenance. At the same time, a cinema earns most of its money during a few busy evening and weekend sessions. If a film performs badly, empty seats cannot be stored and sold later. This is one reason why chains increasingly depend on premium screens, luxury seats and higher food sales. The ordinary ticket is no longer enough to protect every theatre.

D

Property markets add another pressure. Many old theatres occupy central sites that are attractive to developers. A building that struggles as a cinema may be worth more as shops, offices, apartments or a hotel. Some local councils try to protect historic cinema fronts, but preservation is expensive if the auditorium behind the facade has already become unsafe. Campaigners argue that losing a cinema can weaken a town centre, especially in places with few evening activities. Yet owners often say that sentiment cannot pay for repairs, heating or insurance.

E

Not all theatres are disappearing in the same way. Small independent cinemas sometimes survive by becoming cultural venues rather than simple screening rooms. They host film festivals, school events, talks with directors, classic-film nights and community meetings. Some also serve food and drinks, creating a social atmosphere that home viewing cannot reproduce. In areas with strong local support, volunteers may help with ticket sales or fundraising. These cinemas show that the future of movie theatres may depend less on showing every new release than on giving people a reason to gather.

F

The disappearance of theatres is therefore not simply a story of technology replacing an old habit. It is also about land values, changing leisure budgets, film-release strategies and the social role of public spaces. Streaming may explain why many people go less often, but it does not explain why some venues remain loved and busy. The cinema that survives in 2026 is often the one that offers something beyond a screen: comfort, event status, local identity and the feeling that watching a film with strangers is still different from watching alone.

Questions 1-7

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet, write

TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

1 Some early cinemas were designed to make going to the movies feel like a special occasion.
2 The fall in cinema attendance started only after streaming services became popular.
3 Some families improved their home entertainment equipment while cinemas were closed.
4 Cinemas usually earn the same amount of money throughout the week.
5 Local councils always prevent historic cinema buildings from being converted to other uses.
6 Independent cinemas may survive by offering events other than ordinary film screenings.
7 Most cinema volunteers are retired film-industry workers.

Questions 8-13

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet.

Why movie theatres are closing

Older single-screen theatres were often replaced by suburban 8 .................... that could show several films in one location. Today, cinema operators face high 9 ...................., including rent, electricity and staff wages. They must also pay fees to film 10 .................... . If audiences stay away, unsold 11 .................... cannot be kept for another day. In city centres, old theatres may be attractive to property 12 .................... . However, some independent cinemas survive by creating a social 13 .................... that cannot be copied at home.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 1: Some early cinemas were designed to make going to the movies feel like a special occasion.

Correct Answer: TRUE

Accepted Answers: TRUE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Many early cinemas were built as showpieces, with bright signs, decorated ceilings and large foyers designed to make an evening at the pictures feel special.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ TRUE เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Many early cinemas were built as showpieces, with bright signs, decorated ceilings and large foyers designed to make an evening at the pictures feel special.” designed to make going to movies special ตรงกับ designed to make an evening at the pictures feel special ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก TRUE

Paraphrased Vocabulary: going to the movies = an evening at the pictures; special occasion = feel special; answer signal = TRUE

Question 2: The fall in cinema attendance started only after streaming services became popular.

Correct Answer: FALSE

Accepted Answers: FALSE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: The decline did not begin with streaming. Television reduced cinema-going in the mid-twentieth century, and home video later gave audiences another reason to stay away.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ FALSE เพราะข้อความในโจทย์ขัดกับหลักฐานใน passage: “The decline did not begin with streaming. Television reduced cinema-going in the mid-twentieth century, and home video later gave audiences another reason to stay away.” ข้อบอกว่าเริ่มหลัง streaming เท่านั้น แต่ passage บอกชัดว่า decline did not begin with streaming และมี television/home video ก่อนหน้า จุดตัดสินคือ passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับโจทย์ ไม่ใช่แค่ให้รายละเอียดเพิ่ม จึงต้องตอบ FALSE

Paraphrased Vocabulary: fall = decline; started only after streaming = did not begin with streaming; answer signal = FALSE

Question 3: Some families improved their home entertainment equipment while cinemas were closed.

Correct Answer: TRUE

Accepted Answers: TRUE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: During periods when cinemas were closed, families also invested in better screens, speakers and subscriptions.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ TRUE เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “During periods when cinemas were closed, families also invested in better screens, speakers and subscriptions.” improved equipment = invested in better screens, speakers and subscriptions และ while cinemas were closed = during periods when cinemas were closed ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก TRUE

Paraphrased Vocabulary: improved = invested in better; home entertainment equipment = screens, speakers and subscriptions; answer signal = TRUE

Question 4: Cinemas usually earn the same amount of money throughout the week.

Correct Answer: FALSE

Accepted Answers: FALSE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: At the same time, a cinema earns most of its money during a few busy evening and weekend sessions.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ FALSE เพราะข้อความในโจทย์ขัดกับหลักฐานใน passage: “At the same time, a cinema earns most of its money during a few busy evening and weekend sessions.” same amount throughout week ขัดกับ earns most of its money during a few busy evening and weekend sessions จุดตัดสินคือ passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับโจทย์ ไม่ใช่แค่ให้รายละเอียดเพิ่ม จึงต้องตอบ FALSE

Paraphrased Vocabulary: same amount throughout the week = contradicted by most money during a few sessions; answer signal = FALSE

Question 5: Local councils always prevent historic cinema buildings from being converted to other uses.

Correct Answer: FALSE

Accepted Answers: FALSE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Some local councils try to protect historic cinema fronts, but preservation is expensive if the auditorium behind the facade has already become unsafe.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ FALSE เพราะข้อความในโจทย์ขัดกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Some local councils try to protect historic cinema fronts, but preservation is expensive if the auditorium behind the facade has already become unsafe.” always prevent ขัดกับ Some local councils try to protect และยังพูดถึงความยากของ preservation ไม่ใช่ป้องกันได้เสมอ จุดตัดสินคือ passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับโจทย์ ไม่ใช่แค่ให้รายละเอียดเพิ่ม จึงต้องตอบ FALSE

Paraphrased Vocabulary: always prevent = contradicted by some try; historic cinema buildings = historic cinema fronts; answer signal = FALSE

Question 6: Independent cinemas may survive by offering events other than ordinary film screenings.

Correct Answer: TRUE

Accepted Answers: TRUE

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Small independent cinemas sometimes survive by becoming cultural venues rather than simple screening rooms. They host film festivals, school events, talks with directors, classic-film nights and community meetings.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ TRUE เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Small independent cinemas sometimes survive by becoming cultural venues rather than simple screening rooms. They host film festivals, school events, talks with directors, classic-film nights and community meetings.” offering events other than screenings ตรงกับ film festivals, school events, talks, classic-film nights and community meetings ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก TRUE

Paraphrased Vocabulary: independent cinemas = small independent cinemas; ordinary film screenings = simple screening rooms; answer signal = TRUE

Question 7: Most cinema volunteers are retired film-industry workers.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: In areas with strong local support, volunteers may help with ticket sales or fundraising.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage มีข้อมูลใกล้เคียงว่า “In areas with strong local support, volunteers may help with ticket sales or fundraising.” แต่ไม่ได้ยืนยันรายละเอียดเฉพาะที่โจทย์ถาม passage บอกว่ามี volunteers ช่วย ticket sales หรือ fundraising แต่ไม่ได้บอกว่าเป็น retired film-industry workers หรือไม่ ดังนั้นไม่มีหลักฐานพอจะตอบ YES/NO หรือ TRUE/FALSE จึงเป็น NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: volunteers = volunteers; retired film-industry workers = no information; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 8: Older single-screen theatres were often replaced by suburban .................... that could show several films in one location.

Correct Answer: multiplexes

Accepted Answers: multiplexes

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: suburban multiplexes replaced many older single-screen theatres, offering several films under one roof

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ multiplexes เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “suburban multiplexes replaced many older single-screen theatres, offering several films under one roof” replaced by suburban ____ that show several films ตรงกับ suburban multiplexes ... offering several films under one roof คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: one location = under one roof; older single-screen theatres = older single-screen theatres; answer signal = multiplexes

Question 9: Today, cinema operators face high ...................., including rent, electricity and staff wages.

Correct Answer: costs

Accepted Answers: costs

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Operators must pay rent, electricity, staff wages, cleaning costs and fees to film distributors.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ costs เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Operators must pay rent, electricity, staff wages, cleaning costs and fees to film distributors.” rent, electricity and staff wages เป็น costs ที่ operators must pay คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: face high = must pay; including = rent, electricity, staff wages; answer signal = costs

Question 10: They must also pay fees to film .................... .

Correct Answer: distributors

Accepted Answers: distributors

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Operators must pay rent, electricity, staff wages, cleaning costs and fees to film distributors.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ distributors เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Operators must pay rent, electricity, staff wages, cleaning costs and fees to film distributors.” fees to film ____ ตรงกับ fees to film distributors คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: must also pay = must pay; film = film; answer signal = distributors

Question 11: If audiences stay away, unsold .................... cannot be kept for another day.

Correct Answer: seats

Accepted Answers: seats

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: If a film performs badly, empty seats cannot be stored and sold later.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ seats เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “If a film performs badly, empty seats cannot be stored and sold later.” unsold ____ cannot be kept for another day ตรงกับ empty seats cannot be stored and sold later คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: audiences stay away = film performs badly; kept = stored; answer signal = seats

Question 12: In city centres, old theatres may be attractive to property .................... .

Correct Answer: developers

Accepted Answers: developers

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Many old theatres occupy central sites that are attractive to developers.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ developers เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Many old theatres occupy central sites that are attractive to developers.” city centres = central sites และ property ____ = developers คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: old theatres = old theatres; attractive = attractive; answer signal = developers

Question 13: However, some independent cinemas survive by creating a social .................... that cannot be copied at home.

Correct Answer: atmosphere

Accepted Answers: atmosphere

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Disappearance of Movie Theatres

Exact Portion: Some also serve food and drinks, creating a social atmosphere that home viewing cannot reproduce.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ atmosphere เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Some also serve food and drinks, creating a social atmosphere that home viewing cannot reproduce.” creating a social ____ that cannot be copied at home ตรงกับ creating a social atmosphere that home viewing cannot reproduce คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: copied at home = home viewing cannot reproduce; answer signal = atmosphere
